title: General Chemistry 1 Review Study Guide - IB, AP, & College Chem Final Exam
body:
Timestamps:
0:00 Introduction/Overview
1:32 1. Identify # of Subatomic Particles in an Element, and Identify Charge of an Element
3:55 2. Naming Molecular and Ionic Compounds
8:07 3. Percent Composition
11:05 4. Limiting Reagent/Reactant
15:56 5. Molarity, given Grams (19:54 two important formulas)
21:16 6. Dilution
28:57 7. Oxidation States
32:57 8. Titration Problem (includes acid-base neutralization)
41:04 9. Ideal Gas Law
44:09 10. STP, Density
48:13 11. Partial Pressures Question
51:23 12. Identify Unknown Gas
55:45 13. Kinetic Molecular Theory
59:57 14. Deviation From Ideal Gas Law
1:03:47 15. Specific Heat Capacity
1:05:45 16. Heat of Fusion (1:07:23 identify endothermic vs exothermic)
1:08:19 17. Names of Phase Changes
1:10:13 18. Enthalpy of Formation
1:12:23 19. Heat Energy Stoichiometry
1:14:36 20. Energy of a Photon
1:16:22 21. Electron Configuration
1:17:17 22. Quantum Numbers
1:24:23 23. More on Quantum Numbers
1:27:41 24. Energy Levels/Orbitals
1:30:35 Full Chemistry Review
1:31:16 25. Wavelength and Electromagnetic Radiation
1:33:20 26. Ionization Energy (1:35:03 More Periodic Trends)
1:35:38 27. Lewis Structures (Shapes, Angles)
1:39:36 28. Bond Hybridization
1:43:23 29. Polarity
1:47:31 30. Strength of Intermolecular Forces
1:49:23 31. Identify Compound with Strongest Boiling Point
1:51:02 32. Molality, given grams
1:53:02 33. Mass Percent
1:55:53 34. Molarity, given density
1:58:43 35. Boiling Point Elevation (Kb)
2:02:24 36. Osmotic Pressure, Electrolytes
2:05:44 37. Vapor Pressure, Pressure of Solution
2:09:06 38. Average Atomic Mass
2:10:48 39. Percent Yield
2:16:48 40. Find pH

Note: #35-37 may be misnamed. I am taking AP Chemistry, which does not cover colligative properties.

title: General Chemistry 1 Review Study Guide - IB, AP, & College Chem Final Exam
body:
Things you should know about not mentioned in the video:
Know that transition metals require a number for instance CuSO4 would be copper 2 sulfate. Make sure you know what a pure compound is and a pure substance. Also make sure to know what dipoles are and if you have say 10g of C2H4 make sure you can figure out how many moles or grams you have of say carbon or hydrogen. Don't forget about sig figs and how to name acids. ate - ick, ite - ous. Make sure to remember how to identify aqueous solutions and solid solutions. There's videos to remember such as NAG SAG. There's also acronyms to remember strong acids and know that all strong bases have OH in their formula.
author: @dirchhd6985

title: General Chemistry 1 Review Study Guide - IB, AP, & College Chem Final Exam

title: General Chemistry 1 Review Study Guide - IB, AP, & College Chem Final Exam
body: For those wondering at 8, why he simply added 1 and 2 to them. It's because the concentration we are looking for is actually the concentration of H+ ions in the solution, but since there are two moles of H in the molecule we have to add that 2, on the other side since it's a base we have the OH- concentration, however since there is only on mole of OH, the coefficient in front of the m1v1 is well 1. Hope it makes sense, and that I am not mistaken hahaha.

title: General Chemistry 1 Review Study Guide - IB, AP, & College Chem Final Exam
body:
How many protons, electrons, and neutrons are found in the ion shown below? 1:32
What is the correct name for the following compound ‘XXX’? 3:56
calculate the percent composition of aluminum in aluminum sulfite. 8:14
Nitrogen gas reacts with hydrogen gas to form ammonia. Calculate the mass of ammonia produced if 15 g of nitrogen gas reacts with excess hydrogen gas. 11:05
15 g of sodium hydroxide is dissolved in enough water to produce a 250 mL solution. Calculate the molarity of the solution. 16:02
How many mL of water must be added to 200 mL of a 0.75M solution of NaOH to dilute the concentration to 0.25M? 22:21
What is the correct oxidation state of chromium in sodium dichromate? 26:00
38.6 mL of a 0.249M NaOH solution was required to completely titrate 44.7 mL of a sulfuric acid solution. Determine the unknown concentration of the sulfuric acid solution. 32:59
A 250 mL sample of argon gas has a pressure of 1.25 ATM at a temperature of 300 K. Calculate the new pressure if the temperature is increased to 500K and the volume is decrease to 100 mL. 41:07
Calculate the density of oxygen gas at STP. 44:13
Calculate the partial pressure of ammonia if 24 g of nitrogen gas reacts with excess hydrogen gas at 298K inside a 2.50L container. 48:17
4.722 g of an unknown gas is collected over water inside a 2.75 L container at 298K. The total pressure inside the container is 749 torr and the vapor pressure of water is 23.76 torr. Determine the identity of the unknown gas. 51:23
Which of the following statements is not correct. (Gas laws). 55:47
Which of the following conditions will allow a real gas to behave more like an ideal gas? 59:58
How much energy is required to heat 75 g of water from 25 Celsius to 74 Celsius? Specific specific heat capacity of water is 4.184 J/g C. 1:03:51
How much heat energy is required to melt 25.0 g of ice? The heat fusion for ice is 6.01 kJ per mole. 1:05:47
Which of the following represents the phase change from a gas to a solid. 1:08:31
Calculate the enthalpy of the combustion of ethanol using the following information. 1:10:19
Calculate the amount of heat energy released if 10.5 g of propane reacts with excess oxygen gas according to the following chemical equation. 1:12:26
Calculate the energy of a photon that has a wavelength of 451 nm. 1:14:39
What is the ground state electron configuration of fluorine? 1:16:23
Which of the following four sets of quantum numbers correspond to the last electron found in Ni? 1:17:20
Which of the following four sets of quantum numbers are incorrect? 1:24:24
How many orbitals are in the n=4 principle energy level? 1:27:45
Which of the following choices correctly ranks the different forms of electromagnetic radiation in order of increasing wavelength? 1:31:17
Which of the following elements has the highest first ionization energy? 1:33:24
Which of the following molecules has a trigonal planar molecular geometry? 1:35:40
Which of the following molecules has SP3 hybridization around the central atom? 1:39:37
Which of the following molecules is polar? 1:43:26
Rank the following intermolecular forces in order of decreasing strength. 1:47:32
Which of the following molecules has the highest boiling point? 1:49:23
25.0 g of NaOH is dissolved in 300 g of water. Calculate the molality of the solution. 1:51:06
Calculate the molality of a 24% HCl aqueous solution. 1:53:07
Determine the molarity of a 27% HI solution with a density of 1.21 g/mL. 1:55:55
Determine the boiling point of a 1.24m aqueous solution of AlCl3. The Kb for water is 0.512C/m. 1:58:47
32.0 g of an unknown nonelectrolyte solute was dissolved in 345 mL of solution. The osmotic pressure of the resulting solution is 27.1 ATM at 298K. Calculate the molar mass of the unknown solute. 2:02:24
35.1 g of NaCl is dissolved in 200 g of water. The vapor pressure of pure water is 23.76 torr at 25C. Calculate the vapor pressure of the solution. 2:05:44
Calculate the average atomic mass of boron if the relative percent abundance of isotopes B-10 and B-11 are 19% and 81% respectively. 2:09:08
15.5 g of magnesium metal reacts with excess nitrogen gas to produce 18.2 g of magnesium nitride. Calculate the percent yield of magnesium nitride. 2:10:48
Calculate the pH of a 5.43×10^-3 M solution of Ba(OH)2. 2:16:49

title: First-Class Amtrak Train Sleeper and Bedroom Review with Cost in 2026 | Problems with Amtrak
body:
Note: We contacted the customer service department of Amtrak with our issues. We did not tell them we were travel bloggers because we wanted to see what their response would be for the average customer. The woman we spoke to was apologetic and said our issues were unacceptable and that every department involved would be told of our complaint. She added that everyone has a job at Amtrak because of customers like us who buy tickets. Because we had so many issues, they offered a $600 credit for a future train journey, but after a small bit of pushback she came back with $650. Hopefully this video will get shared and be seen by Amtrak management. John and Bev
https://www.facebook.com/share/g/17UuyHR3ut/
author: @RetirementTravelers

title: First-Class Amtrak Train Sleeper and Bedroom Review with Cost in 2026 | Problems with Amtrak
body: My wife and I made plans 8 months in advance for our 50 th anniversary. Cross country on Amtrak 2 weeks before departure Amtrak called and canceled our trip because they were taking the sleeper cars out of service to refurbish them, in the middle of June (peak season) at least we got a total refund , but they did offer a coach car and the seat reclined a bit, i just laughed and hung up on them. If they didnt get government money they would have gone out decades ago
author: @heknows5418

title: First-Class Amtrak Train Sleeper and Bedroom Review with Cost in 2026 | Problems with Amtrak
body: $5 GRAND??? Honestly folks if they didn’t clean the toilets they sure as hell didn’t clean the bedding! That calls for a FIVE THOUSAND DOLLAR REFUND for their damn money grab. Shame on Amtrak.
author: @rayrocker5150

title: First-Class Amtrak Train Sleeper and Bedroom Review with Cost in 2026 | Problems with Amtrak
body: If I paid for a ticket that included a bathroom but the bathroom wasn't working, that would be an immediate deal breaker and demand for a full refund.
author: @user-uv4mg4ve9p

title: First-Class Amtrak Train Sleeper and Bedroom Review with Cost in 2026 | Problems with Amtrak
body: Your Feedback was Respectful and Nicely Done.  I think Everyone including Amtrak should Agree that you did Not Receive your $ 4,700 Worth of Value. 🤔👎
author: @kens.3729

title: First-Class Amtrak Train Sleeper and Bedroom Review with Cost in 2026 | Problems with Amtrak
body: This is because Amtrak, despite reforming management years ago, remains in a paradigm of "government work." It is essentially still a public employment entity with protected employees, just like any government dept.  Someone along the way is saying, "it's not my job"
author: @2TrackMind-c6i

title: First-Class Amtrak Train Sleeper and Bedroom Review with Cost in 2026 | Problems with Amtrak
body: The first time I go to a restaurant, the first thing I do, even before asking for a table, is go to the restroom. If it isn’t extremely clean, I don’t eat there, because if they don’t take care of an area every customer has access to, I don’t want to think about what an area that customers don’t see, such as the kitchen looks like.
author: @georgesouthwick7000

title: First-Class Amtrak Train Sleeper and Bedroom Review with Cost in 2026 | Problems with Amtrak
body: Im retired and was thinking a cross-country train trip would be a great experience.  Thanks for changing my mind!  This was an outstanding review!
author: @ctyankee3001

title: First-Class Amtrak Train Sleeper and Bedroom Review with Cost in 2026 | Problems with Amtrak
body: Every single time we think about booking an Amtrak train across the country, we see a video like this and we change our minds. Yours is an honest review (much appreciated) and others are too (sometimes), but most people pain Amtrak as a wonderland. Thanks again for this review.
author: @rseymour1295

title: First-Class Amtrak Train Sleeper and Bedroom Review with Cost in 2026 | Problems with Amtrak
body: My wife & I often have a roommate or bedroom on 2-3 trips a year. Usually the Starlight from LA to Eugene or Seattle. We’ve never had to strip our beds or gather trash!  It must have been the crew.  A toilet has occasionally had a problem, but never the whole car.  Popular menu items might run low toward the end of a trip, but we’ve always been offered “exchanges/alternatives”.  I still would blame the crews of those trains and hope the hospitality head is fired.  I’m still riding Amtrak knowing that everywhere about 2% of the people cause you 98% of the problems, and that’s where management/supervisors are paid to step in.  Wishing you wonderful trips in the future!
author: @RussSmith-xu6kd

title: First-Class Amtrak Train Sleeper and Bedroom Review with Cost in 2026 | Problems with Amtrak
body: Thank you for the honest review, very helpful. Amtrak's $650 on a future train journey is unacceptable. Have always wanted to do a train journey across the states but boy really re-thinking that idea. Again, thank you for reviews. Safe travels.
author: @dgrove5887

title: First-Class Amtrak Train Sleeper and Bedroom Review with Cost in 2026 | Problems with Amtrak
body: Yours was the first video I encountered as I imagined taking this journey with my mother... I am so thankful I saw your video and will not make the mistake of giving Amtrak our money.  Thank you!
author: @BrotherThadeus

title: First-Class Amtrak Train Sleeper and Bedroom Review with Cost in 2026 | Problems with Amtrak
body: My Dad, a WWII vet, took Amtrak for many years, the Sunset Limited. He had great memories of train trips after the war and all of the people that he met on those trips. Many years later, he would come to visit me in Austin and I would take him to the train in San Antonio where he would board, bound for Florida to visit his brother. I cannot remember a single trip, and he took many, that was on time or without some incident along the way. Generally they were all nightmarish. I cannot understand Amtrak at all, it's not like it's an economical alternative. That it stays afloat is beyond me. I have travelled all over Europe and Africa on trains and they are a pleasure. I don't get Amtrak.
author: @lesrunmore

title: First-Class Amtrak Train Sleeper and Bedroom Review with Cost in 2026 | Problems with Amtrak
body: I traveled from Jasper to Vancouver on VIA rail over a Christmas period, we had a sleeper cabin, food was fantastic, bar and viewing car was excellent. We had a fantastic experience.
author: @joeoldskoolbeef

title: First-Class Amtrak Train Sleeper and Bedroom Review with Cost in 2026 | Problems with Amtrak
body: Thank you SO MUCH for this review.  I think most people would think traveling 1st class wouldn't give so many problems.  I've heard more bad things about Amtrak then good.  Well, it's not Eurorail in Europe.  Thanks again,  Ed
author: @EdAvon-p4d

title: First-Class Amtrak Train Sleeper and Bedroom Review with Cost in 2026 | Problems with Amtrak
body: Several years ago, I took a similar trip with AMTRAK.  My disappointing experience was very much like yours. AMTRAK has improved some, but my thoughts now are "never again".

title: First-Class Amtrak Train Sleeper and Bedroom Review with Cost in 2026 | Problems with Amtrak
body: My husband traveled from Kansas to Flagstaff several years ago. Coach class. He had traveled all over China and Europe on trains. He got off and was really hungry. No opportunity for food, restroom broken in his car, but the seats were good. I am not surprised at your report. For the amount the federal gov puts into these trains, it makes you wonder where the money goes.

title: likes and dislikes of SYRACUSE UNIVERSITY
body: I went to SU...undergrad and grad school. It needs less sports and more serious academics!  Party school is no joke. It is ridiculous! The Mount! All concrete block living! You couldn’t force me to live there! They used to have cottages. Actual houses. Loved those. Some of the majors were great! Newhouse Grad school is the tops. Good law school, but too many majors that are too easy. More money for academics, less for SPORTS!!!

title: Prince and The Revolution - Purple Rain (Live in Syracuse, March 30, 1985)
body:
Purple Rain is very special to me. My Daughter was born the day this song was released. I bought her every purple thing that I could find. Clothes, little shoes, hats and toys, bedding and curtains. We had an early birthday party for my son on Sunday. His birthday was actually on Tuesday. Someone at his party, had a rare virus and apparently kissed my daughter. The next morning, she didn't feel well, by that evening, she was in shock. Doctors in the E.R. couldn't find the issue in time. JoAnna passed away on Tuesday, my son's 3rd. Birthday.

I dressed her in her new purple dress, socks and hat. As she held her purple Barney stuffed animal, I gently kissed her forehead and closed the lid on her tiny white casket. JoAnna was 14 months old. I miss my little Princess more and more, each and every day, more than words can ever say. 😢💜
author: @KevTech1.0

title: Prince and The Revolution - Purple Rain (Live in Syracuse, March 30, 1985)
body: This is by far the best performance of Purple Rain. There will never be another.  This man was a genius.
author: @tneary8319

title: Prince and The Revolution - Purple Rain (Live in Syracuse, March 30, 1985)
body: You Think your a Tough Guy until This tune brings you to Tears. Most soulful performance ever. Prince played for the audience and leaves it all out there.

title: Prince and The Revolution - Purple Rain (Live in Syracuse, March 30, 1985)
body: Its impossible not to get emotional hearing this. We  lost one of the greatest musicians to ever walk this earth.
author: @javiermendoza2942

title: Prince and The Revolution - Purple Rain (Live in Syracuse, March 30, 1985)
body: I will Never Forget This Performance !!  I was There ! I was T-Shirt Security at this show and will never forget how Aw Struck the Entire place was during this Amazing rendition . Ran into a couple girls i knew from Fort Plain NY and escorted them right up front for a few moments as they wer in tears and shaking ! Great Memories . And Now at 62 Years Old its so great to see this clip and still feel the chills ! RIP Prince  <3
author: @RichGillen

title: Prince and The Revolution - Purple Rain (Live in Syracuse, March 30, 1985)
body: The guitar makes us cry ! No words to describe this performance. There will never be nothing close to this in the present or future. Absolute genius ! The guitar speaks with us...shows the pain, the joy...everything ! Magnificent !

title: Prince and The Revolution - Purple Rain (Live in Syracuse, March 30, 1985)

title: Prince and The Revolution - Purple Rain (Live in Syracuse, March 30, 1985)
body:
A thoughtful gift Prince left us... Imagine this song out there, spreading all over the universe through those infinite waves... until the very last second. For ever and ever.
Absolutely beautiful.
author: @raradis

title: Prince and The Revolution - Purple Rain (Live in Syracuse, March 30, 1985)
body: My father was a huge huge prince fan , and from such a young age I remember him playing this song on the guitar over and over until he could perfect it , so it would sound just like Prince. He never thought he was as good him, and maybe I’m a little biased but I thought it sounded exactly like Prince. My dad passed 2 years ago  and listening to this just made me feel like he was in the room here with me , playing his guitar. Music has such power and Prince will live on forever through his music!!I have my dad  to thank for showing me such a musical genius! RIP ,to who in my book, are two great legends! Love you dad ❤️
author: @AlamedaAlien

title: WAS GRAD SCHOOL HONESTLY WORTH IT | SYRACUSE UNIVERSITY
body: I am so glad I found your video. I am currently halfway through with completing my bachelor's degree in digital com, but I feel like my college is not preparing me for my dream career path. What I really want to get into is working in broadcast televisions and hopefully eventually become a producer. I have been doing some research on grad school and perusing through youtube for videos of students sharing their experiences. So far I am an assistant editor at my university's newspaper, in their digital media department. I really am planning on applying to Newhouse, but I wasn't sure which program was the best. Thank you for sharing your words of wisdom and your personal experiences. I wish you the best of luck in all your future career endeavors!
author: @katier4143

title: WAS GRAD SCHOOL HONESTLY WORTH IT | SYRACUSE UNIVERSITY

It's so nice to be able to relate to you and your j-school journey as I am currently looking into programs right now. Good luck with your future and congratulations on your accomplishments so far <33

title: WAS GRAD SCHOOL HONESTLY WORTH IT | SYRACUSE UNIVERSITY
body:
Hi Claribel,
Thank you for sharing your experience. I am really interested in a few Graduate programs offered by Newhouse. But I wasn't sure of any of them are considered STEM eligible. As an international student, the OPT extension would help me greatly in working to recover the amount I would be investing on these programs. Would you be able to tell if any of them are considered STEM eligible? I've been reaching out to the university but am not receiving any response and the application deadlines are coming close.
TIA!
